en.biology: A member of the taxonomic group (tribe Hominini) comprising modern humans, extinct human species, and their immediate fossil ancestors since the divergence from the lineage leading to chimpanzees — a strictly taxonomic/phylogenetic category, not an agency- or intentionality-bearing one.

examples: Homo sapiens, Homo neanderthalensis, and Australopithecus afarensis are all hominins.
nonExamples: Chimpanzees and other non-human great apes are close relatives but not hominins under the tribe-level definition used here.
